title: Photos: ‘No Kings’ sparks protests in Europe
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: 'No Kings' protests spread to Europe with demonstrations in Madrid, Paris, Rome, and Amsterdam on March 28, 2026.
The movement originated in the U.S. against Donald Trump's second term starting January 2025, with mass protests planned nationwide.
Massachusetts hosts over 160 'No Kings' protests, making it the second most active state, following California.

title: Bravo Just Dropped the first full “Real Housewives of Rhode Island” Trailer. Here’s everything we learned.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: 'The Real Housewives of Rhode Island' premieres April 2 at 9 p.m. on Bravo and moves to its regular slot on April 5.
The show features Alicia Carmody, a restaurateur from Providence, and Liz McGraw, a cannabis dispensary owner dubbed 'Cannabis Queen.'
The series explores Rhode Island social dynamics and family life, highlighting community roots and interpersonal relationships within the cast.

title: Hampden County Sheriff plans dozens of job cuts
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Hampden County Sheriff's Department to cut 50 jobs, aiming to save $4 million after a $26.5 million budget reduction.
Sheriff Nick Cocchi warns of reduced police presence in Springfield, West Springfield, Holyoke, and Palmer due to staffing cuts.
Budget issues stem from years of structural problems since abolition of county government in 1999, not department overspending.

title: Quincy approves money for new firefighting gear after PFAS found in old gear
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy approved $2.65 million for new firefighting gear after tests showed old gear contained cancer-linked PFAS.
Over 270 Quincy firefighters will receive PFAS-free gear in response to health concerns and community pressure.
PFAS-free gear purchase was delayed over cost and durability concerns; Massachusetts to ban PFAS in firefighting gear.

title: With his new doc  ‘Henry David Thoreau,’ Don Henley returns to Walden
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Don Henley, Eagles co-founder, produces 'Henry David Thoreau' documentary airing on PBS, highlighting Thoreau's life and environmental legacy.
The Walden Woods Project, founded by Henley in 1990, aims to preserve the land and legacy of Thoreau's Walden Woods.
The documentary features narration by George Clooney and includes voiceovers by Meryl Streep, Jeff Goldblum, and Ted Danson.

title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics attended a friendly soccer match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium during their off day.
Kylian Mbappe scored as France defeated Brazil 2-1; Celtics rookie Hugo Gonzalez swapped jerseys with Mbappe.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla noted similarities between basketball and soccer during the team's bonding experience.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Boston Celtics defeated Atlanta Hawks 109-102 with Payton Pritchard scoring a game-high 36 points.
Jayson Tatum contributed 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists, despite shooting 8-for-24 from the field.
With Jaylen Brown out, Jordan Walsh played notable defensive minutes, blocking three shots during the game.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned this season, ten months after Achilles surgery, motivated by Celtics' strong playoff position.
Tatum, cleared by medical staff, chose to fight through recovery to support Celtics' title contention efforts.
Tatum contributes despite lower shooting averages, excelling in rebounding and versatile play since his comeback.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A man was killed in a shooting at a Lowell apartment complex on Pawtucket Boulevard, says Middlesex DA's Office.
The shooting occurred after an altercation between two armed men who knew each other, according to investigators.
The incident is being investigated by the Middlesex DA’s Office, Massachusetts State Police, and Lowell Police Department.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston for the 2027 season after being purchased by the Houston Rockets owners.
The team is expected to be renamed the Houston Comets, reviving the former WNBA team disbanded in 2008.
The franchise sale reportedly sets a WNBA record at $300 million, coinciding with a new league collective bargaining agreement.
